text.title: Continuous Discovery: Severity Rubric, Rules, and Gotchas
text.summary: Severity rubric for signal prioritization, source-decay rules, anti-hallucination constraints, best practices, and AI-agent gotchas for continuous market scanning.

rule.statement: Apply severity 0-5 to every signal: 5 = pricing/packaging move; 4 = positioning shift / new ICP; 3 = new feature in core wedge; 2 = peripheral feature; 1 = blog/PR; 0 = cosmetic.
rule.rationale: Without a severity rubric, every blog post registers as "competitor activity" and buries severity-5 pricing moves in noise.

rule.statement: Every severity-4+ signal must produce a "respond / monitor / ignore" decision in the next weekly cycle, written into threat-board.md.
rule.rationale: A continuously scanned market that nobody acts on becomes a research graveyard and burns tokens without strategic value.

rule.statement: Require a quarterly source-health audit. When a source goes 30 days without change or returns errors 3 consecutive runs, escalate to human audit and log in dead-source.md.
rule.rationale: SaaS competitors disable RSS, hide changelogs behind login, and move to gated Slack — the scan rots silently without detection.

rule.statement: Capture pricing from at least 2 geos + logged-out + incognito. Require ≥2 geo confirmations before flagging a price change.
rule.rationale: Pricing pages A/B-test by region and cookie — single-shot scrapes give a misleading reading.

rule.statement: Diff content blocks, not raw DOM. Use readability/trafilatura extraction before diffing to strip nav, footer, and cookie banners.
rule.rationale: Boilerplate generates 90% of false-positive diffs; full-DOM diff makes cosmetic CSS changes register as full-page changes.

rule.statement: Pin one strategic Outcome before any scan. Without it, agents track 40 competitors instead of the 5 that move the SOM.
rule.rationale: Agents cheerfully expand scope unless capped by an explicit outcome anchor.

rule.statement: Maintain a versioned competitor-registry.json. All additions/removals via PR — never silent edits — to tie signals to stable competitor_id values.
rule.rationale: Signal correlation across cadences requires stable IDs; silent registry edits break the audit trail.

antipattern.description: LLM hallucinating competitor pricing or changelog entries when scrapes fail, reporting "$49 → $99 price hike" without a source.
antipattern.reason: Without source_url + fetched_at + raw_hash triple enforced at schema level, fabricated diffs are indistinguishable from real ones. Enforce the triple and reject completions missing any field.

antipattern.description: Daily haiku watchers overwriting shared files instead of appending.
antipattern.reason: Concurrent agents writing to the same file produce corrupt outputs. Use atomic JSONL append + lock file, never Write on shared logs.

antipattern.description: Bi-weekly synthesis with full week's signals exceeding context window.
antipattern.reason: Pre-summarize per-day with haiku, feed only summaries to opus for synthesis — never pass raw haiku dumps directly to the synthesizer.

antipattern.description: Review-mining agent asserting "competitor X has churn" from headlines rather than verbatim quotes.
antipattern.reason: Require quote-level evidence (verbatim text + source URL) before any sentiment claim — summary paraphrases hallucinate sentiment direction.

antipattern.description: "New entrant" agent over-triggering on rebrands of existing players.
antipattern.reason: Match on domain + founders + funding history, not just name. A rebrand of a known competitor is not a new entrant.
